danger_signals.visual_form: Devastated, corrupted planetary environments with burnt/destroyed terrain
danger_signals.meaning: World under attack by hostile force; civilization collapse in progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Spatial distortion effects, darkened star system backdrops
danger_signals.meaning: Extreme environmental hazard; escape route is perilous

danger_signals.visual_form: Smoking, charred vehicle wreckage on dune world
danger_signals.meaning: United Freedom Forces defeated; player is isolated survivor

danger_signals.visual_form: Dense machine/robotic enemy spawn patterns across levels
danger_signals.meaning: Military overwhelm; The Machine's army in overwhelming numbers

danger_signals.visual_form: Barren, desert terrain with minimal vegetation or structures
danger_signals.meaning: Civilization destroyed; resource scarcity; hostile alien environment

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character equipped with advanced combat gear and grappling arm
safety_signals.meaning: Player is prepared and equipped; capable of survival and combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Dune buggy, space motorcycle, and underwater motojet pickups
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental traversal solutions; increased mobility equals tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items scattered throughout levels, accessible via grappling arm
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; player agency in maintaining combat readiness

safety_signals.visual_form: Three U.S.S. Avalon assault squad ships departing for mission
safety_signals.meaning: Player is part of larger military organization with backup/support structure

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Advanced military technology visible in spacecraft design and Turrican assault suit aesthetics; high-tech military base (hangar) implied in narrative

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robotic/mechanical enemy designs; phasers and energy weapons; corrupted/metallic aesthetic in affected environments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Devastated planets; corrupted terrain; mechanical stronghold construction; transformation of captured civilization into 'army stronghold'

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilizations thriving; star system functional; normal trade/commerce implied

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Burnt, corrupted planetary surfaces; destroyed infrastructure; barren dune world; smoking wreckage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilizations destroyed instantly; defenses overwhelmed; 'menacing hordes' active; 'tiny cries for help' from survivors; enemy spawn density indicates overwhelming force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dune world completely devastated; black hole proximity; star system 'fell under the spell of evil'; mechanical stronghold construction underway
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: The Machine attempting permanent conquest and transformation; fortress-building behavior; conversion of civilization into military stronghold

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single shipwreck on hostile dune world; isolated player character surrounded by enemy territory; varied battlefield settings indicating contested zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lone survivor fighting against mechanized army; grappling/traversal mechanics indicate environmental navigation challenges; vehicle sections suggest territory control

environmental_storytelling.example: Barren dune world setting conveys civilization collapse; lack of structures/life indicates total destruction of indigenous populations
Advanced Turrican suit and vehicles vs. devastated primitive environment shows high-tech military response to catastrophic invasion
Multiple planets and environmental types (dune, underwater, space) suggest expanding The Machine's conquest across entire star system; player must traverse through zones of occupation
Mechanical/robotic enemy aesthetics visually communicate The Machine's nature as non-organic, automated threat
Desert buggy, motorcycle, and motojet indicate environmental hazards player must overcome; vehicle mechanics reflect world state (need for specialized traversal)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Launching phasers from deep space; destroying civilizations instantly; establishing military stronghold; transforming captured territory into army fortress
Launching three-ship assault team from U.S.S. Avalon flagship; navigating dangerous black hole; attempting to reach attacked system; fielding elite Turrican operative
Shipwrecked; forced to fight alone; utilizing grappling arm for environmental interaction; piloting multiple vehicle types; advancing through multiple level zones
Destroyed/extinct (implied); sending distress signals across system boundaries; incapable of self-defense against The Machine

notes: Super Turrican 2 communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing and environmental visual devastation rather than dynamic HUD elements. The 'run and gun' paradigm mirrors military invasion scenario—player must traverse hostile territory eliminating enemy forces. Mode 7 effects and varied visual settings create sense of multiple conquered zones. Gameplay mechanics (grappling arm, vehicles) directly reflect environmental hazards of devastated world. Story is conveyed through mission briefing context rather than in-game visual feedback systems. Faction identity is implicit: United Freedom Forces represented by advanced military technology and Turrican suit design; The Machine represented by mechanical enemy aesthetics and corrupted landscape. The black hole environment establishes existential danger—even escape route is lethal. Overall visual language emphasizes isolation, devastation, and overwhelming odds through environment design rather than explicit UI danger signals.
sources_used: wikipedia - Super Turrican 2 (Story section, Gameplay section)
